name: Supercharge /t/{domain} Campaign Creator
description: Transform the URL campaign creator from a single-scrape-and-guess flow into a multi-source intelligence powerhouse. Wire EXA, AI Ark, Apollo, Apify, and Perplexity into the enrichment pipeline. Add real prospect intelligence, competitive battlecards, multi-touch sequences, and a live research feed loading experience.

stories.title: Create demo-research-v2 edge function with multi-source parallel enrichment
acceptance: New edge function demo-research-v2 fires 4 parallel enrichment calls: EXA neural search, AI Ark company search, Apollo org enrich, and website scrape
All 4 calls use Promise.allSettled with individual 10s timeouts - partial data is fine
Results merged into a single context blob passed to Gemini 2.5 Flash for structured extraction
Returns the same ResearchData shape as demo-research (backward compatible)
Falls back gracefully: if EXA/AI Ark/Apollo fail, Gemini still works with whatever data arrived
stories.notes: Reuse existing adapters: exaSearch.ts for EXA, AI Ark uses direct fetch to api.ai-ark.com/api/developer-portal/v1/companies (X-TOKEN auth, env var AI_ARK_API_KEY). Apollo uses apollo-org-enrich pattern. Website scrape is the existing fetch+clean from demo-research. Gemini synthesis prompt gets ALL source data concatenated with source labels. Use env vars for API keys (already set in staging/prod). Cost per call: ~$0.01 total (EXA $0.005 + AI Ark 2.5 credits + Apollo ~$0.003 + Gemini ~$0.001).

stories.title: Extend ResearchData type with firmographic and intelligence fields
acceptance: ResearchData.company gains: funding_stage, funding_total, revenue_range, tech_stack[], leadership_team[], recent_news[], founded_year, headquarters
New ResearchData.competitive field: competitors[] with {name, domain, differentiators[]}
New ResearchData.prospect field: {title, seniority, linkedin_url, photo_url, recent_activity[], department}
All new fields are optional (existing data flows unaffected)
demo-data.ts mock generator updated to include sample data for new fields
stories.notes: Keep backward compatible. Every new field is optional with ?. The mock data should have realistic examples so the loading animation can show them. generateResearchFromUrl needs updating too.

stories.title: Wire CreatorView to use demo-research-v2 and pass enriched data
acceptance: useDemoResearch hook calls demo-research-v2 instead of demo-research
Fallback: if v2 returns error, retry with original demo-research endpoint
Agent animation findings updated to show real firmographic data (funding, tech stack, news)
New fields passed through to OutreachComposer and SandboxExperience
stories.notes: Minimal change: swap the endpoint URL in fetchResearch(), update buildRealAgentFindings() to show funding/tech stack/news in agent findings. Everything else flows through the existing ResearchData pipe.

stories.title: Create prospect-enrich edge function for person-level intelligence
acceptance: New edge function prospect-enrich accepts {email?, linkedin_url?, first_name?, last_name?, domain?}
Fires parallel: AI Ark reverse-lookup (email) + Apollo people/match (email+domain) + EXA search (person LinkedIn activity)
Returns: {title, seniority, department, linkedin_url, photo_url, company_name, recent_activity[], interests[]}
Auth-gated: requires valid JWT
Graceful degradation: returns whatever fields were found, nulls for the rest
stories.notes: Follow enrich-cascade pattern for AI Ark (X-TOKEN auth, /v1/people/reverse-lookup with kind: CONTACT, search: {email}). Apollo uses /v1/people/match with {email, domain}. EXA search: query person name + company for LinkedIn posts/articles (date filter last 6 months). Merge: AI Ark primary, Apollo fills gaps. Cost: ~$0.015 per prospect (AI Ark 1.25 credits + Apollo 1 credit + EXA $0.005).

stories.title: Show prospect intelligence card in OutreachComposer
acceptance: When query params include email or fn+ln+domain, CreatorView calls prospect-enrich in parallel with demo-research-v2
OutreachComposer prospect card shows: photo (or initials avatar), full name, title, seniority, LinkedIn link
If recent_activity found, show 1-2 bullet points (e.g. 'Posted about scaling accounting teams 2 weeks ago')
Prospect data passed to campaign-outreach-draft for hyper-personalized drafts
Loading skeleton while prospect enrichment runs
stories.notes: The prospect card replaces the simple 'Prospect: John Smith john@capium.com' box with a rich intelligence card. Pass prospect data as a new field in the campaign-outreach-draft request body. The draft prompt can reference their actual title, recent posts, and interests.

stories.title: Upgrade campaign-outreach-draft to use full enrichment context
acceptance: campaign-outreach-draft accepts optional prospect_intel field with title, seniority, recent_activity, interests
Gemini prompt references prospect's actual role, recent LinkedIn activity, and interests when available
Company section of prompt includes funding stage, tech stack, recent news from enriched data
Draft quality measurably better: references specific prospect activity, not just company vertical
Backward compatible: works fine without prospect_intel (existing behavior unchanged)
stories.notes: Extend the Gemini prompt ABOUT THE PROSPECT section. Add: 'PROSPECT INTELLIGENCE: Title: {title}, Recent activity: {activity}, Interests: {interests}. Reference their specific role and recent activity in the message.' Also enrich the company section with funding/news. Keep the dead language rules intact.

stories.title: Add competitive intelligence panel to SandboxExperience
acceptance: New CompetitiveIntel card in SandboxApp pipeline/deal view showing real competitors from enrichment
Each competitor shows: name, logo (via img.logo.dev/{domain}), 2-3 differentiator bullet points
If competitive data empty, card hidden (not broken placeholder)
Card styled consistently with existing SandboxApp deal signals cards
Works in both CreatorView preview (left pane) and ProspectView
stories.notes: This is the 'holy shit' moment in the demo. Use AI Ark similarity search data (from demo-research-v2 which calls it) for real lookalike companies. Logo URLs: https://img.logo.dev/{domain}?token=... (already used in IntegrationLogo component). generatePersonalizedData needs to pass competitive data through to SandboxData.

stories.title: Generate multi-touch outreach sequence (3 emails) instead of single draft
acceptance: campaign-outreach-draft accepts optional mode: 'single' (default, existing) or 'sequence'
Sequence mode returns 3 touches: {touches: [{day: 0, subject, body}, {day: 3, subject, body}, {day: 7, subject, body}]}
Touch 1: Personalized intro + demo link (uses prospect intel + company data)
Touch 2: Value-add follow-up referencing recent news/funding from EXA enrichment
Touch 3: Break-up email with social proof angle
Each touch uses different enrichment data so emails don't feel templated
stories.notes: Follow the generate-email-sequence pattern: single Gemini call with all 3 touches in one prompt (cheaper and more coherent than 3 separate calls). The prompt should specify: Touch 1 references prospect's role/activity, Touch 2 references company news/funding, Touch 3 references competitor landscape. Use Gemini 2.5 Flash for sequence mode (better reasoning for multi-part). Keep temperature at 0.8.

stories.title: Multi-touch sequence UI in OutreachComposer
acceptance: Toggle switch above channel picker: 'Single message' / 'Sequence (3 touches)'
Sequence mode shows 3 tabs: 'Day 0: Intro' / 'Day 3: Follow-up' / 'Day 7: Break-up'
Each tab has its own editable subject + body textarea
Regenerate button regenerates all 3 touches (clears sequence cache)
Create Link button text changes to 'Create Sequence Link' in sequence mode
stories.notes: The sequence tabs sit inside the existing channel picker context (Email only for now - LinkedIn/Slack stay single message). Cache the full sequence in draftCacheRef. When switching between single/sequence, keep both cached independently. The [LINK] placeholder appears in all 3 touches.

stories.title: Live research feed loading experience with SSE streaming
acceptance: demo-research-v2 supports Accept: text/event-stream header to return SSE stream
Each provider emits an event when it returns: {provider: 'exa', status: 'complete', summary: 'Found: Capium - Cloud accounting...', data_preview: {...}}
Frontend receives events and renders each as an animated line in the loading screen
Each line shows: provider icon + real data summary (not generic 'working...' text)
If SSE fails or browser doesn't support it, falls back to existing polling behavior
stories.notes: Deno supports SSE natively: return new Response(readableStream, {headers: {'Content-Type': 'text/event-stream'}}). Use a TransformStream. Each provider wraps its call and writes to the stream when done. Final event is 'complete' with full merged ResearchData. Frontend uses EventSource or fetch+reader. The loading UI becomes a real-time intelligence feed - this IS the demo of 60's capabilities.

stories.title: Research feed loading UI component with real-time line rendering
acceptance: New ResearchFeed component replaces the spinner in CreatorView loading state
Each SSE event renders as an animated line: [icon] [provider label] [real data summary]
Lines cascade in with framer-motion stagger (100ms between entries)
Provider icons: Search (EXA), Building (AI Ark), Users (Apollo), Globe (Website), Sparkles (Gemini synthesis)
After all providers complete, smooth transition to split-screen view (no flash)
stories.notes: This replaces the current loading state in CreatorView (lines 69-90). The component receives events from useDemoResearch and renders them. Each line shows REAL data: 'EXA: Found Capium - Cloud accounting platform, Series B funded' not 'Researching capium.com...'. Use existing motion.div patterns from the codebase. The feed should feel like watching a terminal with intelligence streaming in.

stories.title: Deploy all new edge functions to staging and verify end-to-end
acceptance: demo-research-v2 deployed to staging with --no-verify-jwt
prospect-enrich deployed to staging with --no-verify-jwt
campaign-outreach-draft redeployed to staging with --no-verify-jwt
Visit /t/capium.com on staging - live research feed shows real EXA/AI Ark/Apollo data
Outreach draft references real prospect data and company intelligence
Full flow: research feed -> split screen -> draft -> create link -> preview works
stories.notes: Deploy command: npx supabase functions deploy {name} --project-ref caerqjzvuerejfrdtygb --no-verify-jwt. Verify API keys are set: EXA_API_KEY, AI_ARK_API_KEY, APOLLO_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY. Test with 3 different domains to verify robustness.

parallelOpportunities.reason: Independent edge functions with no file overlap
Competitive intel panel and sequence generation are independent features
Sequence UI and SSE streaming are independent frontend/backend work

mvp.delivers: Multi-source enrichment + prospect intelligence + hyper-personalized drafts (the core value)

1_multi_source_research.description: Replace single scrape+Gemini with EXA + AI Ark + Apollo + scrape -> Gemini synthesis
1_multi_source_research.impact: 10x richer company data feeding everything downstream

2_prospect_intelligence.description: Enrich the actual prospect via AI Ark + Apollo + EXA for person-level intelligence
2_prospect_intelligence.impact: Outreach references real title, activity, interests instead of generic greeting

3_competitive_battlecard.description: Show real competitive landscape in the demo using AI Ark similarity + EXA
3_competitive_battlecard.impact: Demo feels like a live intelligence briefing, not a template

4_multi_touch_sequence.description: Generate 3-email sequence with escalating personalization instead of single message
4_multi_touch_sequence.impact: Full outreach play: intro + value-add follow-up + break-up

5_live_research_feed.description: Replace loading spinner with SSE-streamed real-time intelligence feed
5_live_research_feed.impact: Loading screen becomes the demo - users watch AI pull real intel in real-time

risks.issue: AI Ark and Apollo API keys may not be set as Supabase secrets for the demo-research context
risks.mitigation: demo-research-v2 uses Promise.allSettled - missing providers are silently skipped. Verify keys exist before deploying.

risks.issue: Adding 3 more API calls could increase total enrichment time
risks.mitigation: All calls are parallel with 10s individual timeouts. EXA returns in 200ms, AI Ark in 500ms, Apollo in 800ms. Worst case adds ~1s to current 4-6s. SSE streaming (SCC-010) makes this invisible.

risks.issue: Each /t/{domain} visit now costs ~$0.025 instead of ~$0.001
risks.mitigation: Still very cheap. Cache research_data in campaign_links table so repeat visits don't re-enrich. Add rate limit (already exists: 10 req/min per IP).

risks.issue: SSE may not work in all browsers or with certain Supabase gateway configs
risks.mitigation: SCC-010 has explicit fallback to existing non-streaming behavior. SSE is progressive enhancement.

risks.issue: New ResearchData fields could break existing ProspectView if not optional
risks.mitigation: SCC-002 makes all new fields optional with ?. Existing flows see undefined and ignore them.

reuse.for: AI Ark + Apollo cascade pattern for prospect-enrich
Lookalike company search for competitive intel

noChanges.reason: Keep as fallback - v2 calls this internally if all providers fail
Orchestrator unchanged - just receives richer data
Schema already has JSONB fields for all new data

apiCosts.fullFlowTotal: ~$0.03 per /t/{domain} visit with prospect enrichment + sequence
